name: string-database
description: Query STRING API for protein-protein interactions (59M proteins, 20B interactions). Network analysis, GO/KEGG enrichment, interaction discovery, 5000+ species, for systems biology.
capabilities: Query protein-protein interaction networks from STRING database
Perform GO/KEGG functional enrichment analysis on protein lists
Generate network visualization images with evidence-based coloring
Map protein identifiers to STRING IDs for accurate queries
Test network connectivity significance with PPI enrichment
Retrieve homology scores between proteins for evolutionary analysis
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: The string-database skill is a legitimate bioinformatics tool for accessing protein-protein interaction data from the STRING database (string-db.org), a trusted ELIXIR resource. All 291 static findings are false positives: backticks in documentation are code formatting, HTTP requests target the official STRING API, file writes are for saving network images, and 'cryptographic' and 'reconnaissance' patterns are misinterpreted scientific terminology.
valueStatement: Researchers need to understand protein-protein interactions to study biological systems and disease mechanisms. This skill provides direct access to STRING's comprehensive database of 59M proteins and 20B+ interactions across 5000+ species.
userTitle: Query protein interaction networks from STRING database
limitations: Requires internet connection to access STRING API
Limited to proteins available in STRING database (5000+ species)
API rate limits apply (1 second between calls recommended)
Large queries may timeout or require batching into smaller groups

useCases.title: Analyze differentially expressed genes
useCases.description: Upload a list of proteins from RNA-seq or proteomics experiments to identify enriched pathways and interaction networks.

useCases.title: Study protein function and interactions
useCases.description: Investigate specific proteins to discover interaction partners, visualize networks, and understand biological roles.

useCases.title: Build and analyze biological networks
useCases.description: Construct comprehensive interaction networks and test if proteins form significant functional modules.

promptTemplates.title: Basic protein network
promptTemplates.scenario: Find interactions for a single protein
promptTemplates.prompt: Get the protein interaction network for TP53 in humans with medium confidence (400), including 5 additional nodes, and save as PNG image.

promptTemplates.title: Functional enrichment
promptTemplates.scenario: Analyze a gene list from experiment
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform functional enrichment analysis on these proteins: TP53, BRCA1, ATM, CHEK2, MDM2. Show GO biological processes with FDR < 0.05.

promptTemplates.title: Cross-species comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Get interaction networks for p53 protein in human (9606) and mouse (10090) with high confidence (700), then compare the top 10 interactors.

promptTemplates.title: Pathway analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this DNA repair protein list: map IDs, get interaction network with 700 confidence, test PPI enrichment, perform GO/KEGG enrichment, and generate evidence-colored network image.

bestPractices: Always map protein identifiers first using string_map_ids for faster and more accurate queries
Use appropriate confidence thresholds: 400 for standard analysis, 700 for high-confidence interactions
Include species parameter (NCBI taxon ID) for networks with more than 10 proteins
antiPatterns: Do not query with more than 100 proteins in a single call - split large lists into batches
Avoid using very low confidence thresholds (< 150) without biological justification
Do not ignore species specification for multi-protein networks

faq.question: What is the STRING database?
faq.answer: STRING is a comprehensive database of protein-protein interactions covering 59M proteins and 20B+ interactions across 5000+ organisms, integrating data from experiments, databases, and text-mining.

faq.question: Which species are supported?
faq.answer: STRING supports 5000+ species. Common ones include human (9606), mouse (10090), rat (10116), fly (7227), yeast (4932), and zebrafish (7955).

faq.question: What confidence threshold should I use?
faq.answer: Use 400 for standard analysis, 700 for high-confidence interactions, and 150 for exploratory analysis. Higher thresholds give fewer but more reliable interactions.

faq.question: How do I cite STRING?
faq.answer: Cite the latest STRING publication from https://string-db.org/cgi/about. The data is freely available under Creative Commons BY 4.0 license.

faq.question: Can I analyze more than 100 proteins?
faq.answer: Yes, but split large lists into batches of 100 or fewer proteins to avoid timeouts and ensure optimal performance.

faq.question: What is the difference between functional and physical networks?
faq.answer: Functional networks include all evidence types (recommended for most analyses). Physical networks only show direct binding evidence (for structural studies).
